title: 2026 Oscars: How One Battle Beat Sinners, Jordan Overcame Chalamet
text: One Battle After Another won Best Picture at the 98th Academy Awards, while Sinners, despite receiving a record 16 nominations, won only 4 awards including Best Actor. Michael B. Jordan's Best Actor win over Timothée Chalamet represented a significant upset given Chalamet's early season dominance with wins at Golden Globes and Critics Choice Awards. The analysis highlights that Chalamet's campaign missteps, including controversial comments about ballet and opera and an overly aggressive self-promotion strategy, contributed to his loss. Jordan's campaign, by contrast, emphasized gratitude and respect for those who came before him, particularly Black actors who won Oscars previously. The Best Actor race demonstrated how precursor award wins don't automatically translate to Oscar victories, and how voter sentiment can shift dramatically in the weeks leading up to the ceremony. Chalamet's youth was also noted as a factor, with some voters suggesting he would have better opportunities in future years. Jordan's win marked a turning point for the film Sinners, establishing it as a major player despite losing the Best Picture race to One Battle After Another, which earned six awards total.
